title: Code of Conduct Lesson #2 — Youth Soccer Referee Abuse (Level 2)
source: Based on U.S. Soccer Policy 531-9, effective March 2025

questions.question: What is the minimum suspension for Non-Physical Level 2 Abuse?

answers.label: A
answers.text: 2-game suspension

answers.label: B
answers.text: 3-game suspension

answers.label: C
answers.text: 4-game suspension

answers.label: D
answers.text: 6-game suspension

questions.question: What is the minimum penalty for Physical Level 2 Abuse?

answers.label: A
answers.text: 4 games plus a warning

answers.label: B
answers.text: 6 games plus 1–6 months ban

answers.label: C
answers.text: 10 games plus 6–24 months ban

answers.label: D
answers.text: Immediate lifetime ban

questions.question: How does Level 2 Abuse differ from Level 1 Abuse in terms of verbal behavior?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Level 2 covers only physical contact; Level 1 covers all verbal behavior

answers.label: B
answers.text: Level 2 involves expletives, personal attacks, intimidation, and relentless pressure, while Level 1 covers insults, mocking, and dismissive gestures

answers.label: C
answers.text: Level 2 is only triggered when a referee files a formal complaint

answers.label: D
answers.text: There is no meaningful distinction — both levels carry the same penalty

questions.question: Shouting 'That call was complete $#@%!' at a referee is classified as what under Policy 531-9?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Level 1 Non-Physical Abuse

answers.label: B
answers.text: Level 2 Non-Physical Abuse — Using Expletives at the Referee

answers.label: C
answers.text: Acceptable emotional expression in a competitive setting

answers.label: D
answers.text: Level 2 Physical Abuse

questions.question: Does it matter how loudly a personal attack with profanity is delivered when applying Policy 531-9?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Yes — only statements loud enough to be heard by others are penalized

answers.label: B
answers.text: Yes — volume determines whether it is Level 1 or Level 2

answers.label: C
answers.text: No — it constitutes abusive language regardless of volume

answers.label: D
answers.text: Only if a minor referee is within earshot

questions.question: What type of Level 2 Abuse is described by storming toward a referee from 20 yards away while shouting?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Physical Level 2 Abuse — Shoving

answers.label: B
answers.text: Non-Physical Level 2 Abuse — Intimidating Body Language

answers.label: C
answers.text: Level 1 Non-Physical Abuse — Mocking

answers.label: D
answers.text: Not abuse — no physical contact was made

questions.question: Under Policy 531-9, can moving aggressively toward a referee without making contact constitute Level 2 Abuse?

answers.label: A
answers.text: No — only actual physical contact triggers a penalty

answers.label: B
answers.text: Only if the referee formally reports feeling threatened

answers.label: C
answers.text: Yes — closing space rapidly or positioning your body in a threatening way qualifies

answers.label: D
answers.text: Only if the approach is combined with verbal profanity

questions.question: A parent makes complaints about every single call for 20+ minutes during a U10 match. How does Policy 531-9 classify this?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Acceptable, as long as no single comment is abusive on its own

answers.label: B
answers.text: Level 1 Abuse because no profanity was used

answers.label: C
answers.text: Level 2 Abuse — Harassment Through Escalating Intensity

answers.label: D
answers.text: Not covered by Policy 531-9 — cumulative behavior is not penalized

questions.question: Is harassment under Policy 531-9 always triggered by a single dramatic incident?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Yes — only a single clearly abusive incident qualifies

answers.label: B
answers.text: No — cumulative relentless pressure over a match is also explicitly covered as Level 2 Abuse

answers.label: C
answers.text: Only if the referee requests a formal review

answers.label: D
answers.text: Yes — pattern-based abuse requires a separate Level 3 classification

questions.question: Saying 'I'm going to report you to the league and make sure you never referee again' during a match is classified as what?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A legitimate exercise of league rights

answers.label: B
answers.text: Level 1 Abuse — Accusations of Bias

answers.label: C
answers.text: Level 2 Abuse — Retaliation and Threatening Consequences

answers.label: D
answers.text: Not abuse — reporting a referee is always permitted

questions.question: What is the appropriate channel for raising genuine concerns about a referee's conduct?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Confront the referee directly at the end of the match

answers.label: B
answers.text: Post about it on social media so the league is aware

answers.label: C
answers.text: Report it through your league after the game through official channels

answers.label: D
answers.text: Gather other parents to present a group complaint during the match

questions.question: A spectator lightly shoves a referee, insisting they 'barely pushed him.' How does Policy 531-9 treat this?

answers.label: A
answers.text: As Level 1 Physical Abuse, since the contact was light

answers.label: B
answers.text: As Level 2 Physical Abuse — intentional confrontation, regardless of force

answers.label: C
answers.text: Not covered unless the referee falls or is injured

answers.label: D
answers.text: The intent must be assessed before a penalty is assigned

questions.question: Following a referee to the parking lot after the match to 'have a word' is classified as what under Policy 531-9?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Acceptable, as long as no threatening language is used

answers.label: B
answers.text: Level 1 Physical Abuse

answers.label: C
answers.text: Level 2 Physical Abuse — Intimidation and Intentional Confrontation

answers.label: D
answers.text: Only an offense if the referee files a formal report

questions.question: Why is following a referee to the parking lot considered a Level 2 Physical Abuse offense even if nothing is said?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Because it delays the referee's departure from the venue

answers.label: B
answers.text: Because it constitutes stalking behavior and creates genuine fear for personal safety

answers.label: C
answers.text: Because the referee is no longer on duty and cannot defend themselves

answers.label: D
answers.text: It is only an offense if the referee is a minor

questions.question: A parent throws a water bottle on the ground — not at the referee — in frustration after a call. Is this covered by Policy 531-9?

answers.label: A
answers.text: No — the object was not thrown at the referee, so no offense occurred

answers.label: B
answers.text: Only if the bottle lands within 5 feet of the referee

answers.label: C
answers.text: Yes — throwing objects near the referee is Level 2 Physical Abuse regardless of intent

answers.label: D
answers.text: Only if it occurs during active play

questions.question: Which of the following actions is explicitly listed as Physical Level 2 Abuse in Policy 531-9?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Tapping the referee's shoulder to get their attention

answers.label: B
answers.text: Giving a thumbs-down gesture at the referee

answers.label: C
answers.text: Forcefully grabbing the referee's arm

answers.label: D
answers.text: Repeatedly calling out 'terrible call' from the sideline

questions.question: What does Policy 531-9 prescribe for a second Level 2 Non-Physical offense?

answers.label: A
answers.text: The same 4-game minimum as the first offense

answers.label: B
answers.text: 8 games — double the first-offense minimum

answers.label: C
answers.text: An automatic lifetime ban

answers.label: D
answers.text: A formal hearing with no automatic game suspension

questions.question: What happens to the Physical Level 2 penalty (10 games) on a second offense?

answers.label: A
answers.text: It increases to 15 games

answers.label: B
answers.text: It increases to 20 games

answers.label: C
answers.text: It stays at 10 games but the ban length doubles

answers.label: D
answers.text: It triggers an automatic lifetime ban

questions.question: What is the consequence for a third Level 2 offense under Policy 531-9?

answers.label: A
answers.text: A 2-year ban from all U.S. Soccer-affiliated matches

answers.label: B
answers.text: A 10-game suspension plus a 24-month ban

answers.label: C
answers.text: Lifetime ban from all U.S. Soccer-affiliated matches, including as a spectator

answers.label: D
answers.text: Referral to a national disciplinary board for further review

questions.question: What automatic penalty multiplier applies when a Level 2 offense is committed against a referee who is a minor?

answers.label: A
answers.text: The penalty doubles

answers.label: B
answers.text: The penalty triples

answers.label: C
answers.text: The standard penalty applies — the referee's age is not a factor

answers.label: D
answers.text: The offender receives an additional written warning

questions.question: The course states that a 4-game suspension harms not just the offender. What broader family impact is highlighted?

answers.label: A
answers.text: The offender's family must pay a fine to the league

answers.label: B
answers.text: The offender is removed from 4 matches their child plays, affecting the whole family

answers.label: C
answers.text: Other family members may also face temporary bans

answers.label: D
answers.text: The child may be suspended from the team by association

questions.question: According to the course, what do young players learn when adults use expletives at referees?

answers.label: A
answers.text: That referees should be held to a high standard

answers.label: B
answers.text: That emotional regulation is optional for adults

answers.label: C
answers.text: That passion for the game is important

answers.label: D
answers.text: That rules exist and must be enforced

questions.question: Which technique does the course recommend to manage frustration before reacting to a call?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Immediately speak to the referee to clarify the call

answers.label: B
answers.text: Use a 5-second pause before reacting

answers.label: C
answers.text: Ask another spectator whether the call was correct

answers.label: D
answers.text: Signal to the coach to dispute the call on your behalf

questions.question: The course recommends 'keeping it internal.' What does this mean in practice?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Discuss concerns only with other parents, never with the coach

answers.label: B
answers.text: File all complaints internally with the league rather than speaking publicly

answers.label: C
answers.text: Muttering under your breath is fine — directing comments at the referee at any volume is not

answers.label: D
answers.text: Keep all opinions to yourself and never react to any call

questions.question: What simple act of sportsmanship does the course encourage toward referees after every match, regardless of what happened during the game?

answers.label: A
answers.text: Submitting written feedback about the referee's performance to the league

answers.label: B
answers.text: Applauding the referee as they leave the field

answers.label: C
answers.text: Thanking the referee for their time

answers.label: D
answers.text: Asking the referee to review any disputed calls before leaving
